context_bar: Three months in this tee. Same shape.
question_text: Do heavy tees actually hold form through a full work day?
answer_text: At 260 GSM the structure is built in, not ironed in. Mine sits the same at 9am and 9pm. No sag at the neckline, no creasing at the waist.

context_bar: Worn it back to back for a week.
question_text: Does a heavier tee mean it traps heat in Indian weather?
answer_text: That was the assumption we refused to accept. The weave is engineered to breathe. Heavy hand, no sweat through. Tested it across May in Bangalore.

context_bar: Stopped reaching for anything else this season.
question_text: Why pay this much for a plain tee?
answer_text: Heavier fabric, longer development, slower drops. You are buying a garment that retains shape across a hundred wears, not one that flattens after ten.
Will the collar still stand after multiple washes?
column4: Yes. Ribbed, reinforced, finished to hold. Mine has been through twenty-plus washes. Sits the same as day one.

context_bar: The drape is what sold me.
question_text: How does the Cloud Tee compare to a regular cotton tee?
answer_text: Two millimetre fluted texture, 260 GSM, structured fall. A regular tee clings and creases. This one keeps its line whether you are sitting, walking, or standing.

context_bar: Wore it under a blazer all day.
question_text: Does it work for office settings or only weekends?
answer_text: Both. The Flow Tee in Supima mercerised cotton has a quiet lustre that reads polished under a jacket. Off the jacket, it still holds presence on its own.

context_bar: The weight is the whole point.
question_text: Is 240 GSM too heavy for daily wear?
answer_text: 240 is the considered middle. Structured enough to drape clean, breathable enough to wear through a full day. It does not feel heavy. It feels resolved.
How do I care for it so it lasts?
column4: Cold wash, inside out, line dry. No fabric softener. The fibre is built to hold, the routine just protects what is already there.

context_bar: The fit is the quietest part of it.
question_text: Is the cut boxy or fitted?
answer_text: Classic. Sits true to the shoulder, falls clean at the hip. Not slim, not oversized. Designed to disappear from your attention once it is on.

context_bar: Mercerised cotton was the upgrade I needed.
question_text: What does mercerised actually mean for a polo?
answer_text: Treated cotton fibres lock in lustre and tensile strength. The result is smoother to the touch, more colour-fast over time, and it holds shape wash after wash.
